title: rubus radula var. rudis
intro: Rubus radula var. rudis, also known as the rough-leaved bramble or wild raspberry, is a variant of the Rubus radula species, a type of bramble or thorny shrub native to New Zealand. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus radula var. rudis include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, as well as its traditional use as a treatment for digestive issues and menstrual cramps. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional and folk medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
